title: Emberfall Crossing
atAGlance: Emberfall Crossing is a cliff-hung market citadel built from salvaged bridge arches, riveted caravan decks, and lift pylons lashed across a volcanic gorge. Its districts stack by purpose: Ledger Tier for contracts and arbitration, Ropecourt for caravan berths and labor auctions, and Cinderwalk for shrines, cookfires, and refugee camps pressed wall to wall. The settlement survives by selling what no fortress can mint alone: neutral ground, verified weights, and enforceable passage promises between enemies who would gladly kill one another beyond the gate chains. Wealth moves quickly here, but so do rumors, blackmail notes, and bounty marks.
sageNotes: Emberfall Crossing survives on enforced courtesy and profitable restraint. In Ledger Tier, contracts and grudges share the same tables; in Ropecourt, berth priority can decide whether a district eats; in Cinderwalk, refugees trade labor, rumor, and heirlooms for one more week behind neutral chains. The major powers are always visible but rarely honest about their aims: Concord factors insure caravans, Basalt Crown buyers strip the markets for steel and medics, and Rootbound envoys trade safe-jungle knowledge for future concessions. Anyone who does business here learns the names quickly: Vell 'Three-Tallies' Marr at Counting House Nine, Magistrate Ilyra Coalsign, and Dock-Chief Harn Balehook. Every bargain in Emberfall costs twice, once in coin and once in obligation.
gameInformation: Use Emberfall for downtime, rumor acquisition, and faction brokerage with a live Leverage system. Start each visit with a Leverage Clock and a Stability Clock. Leverage Clock beats: 2 (price spikes and queue riots), 4 (contract fraud accusations and retaliatory seizures), 6 (open faction militias in Ropecourt). Stability Clock beats: 2 (curfews and panic buying), 1 (sanctuary terms publicly challenged), 0 (Emberfall fractures into armed enclaves and travel costs double). Social encounter procedure: define objective (secure passage, clear warrant, buy intelligence, broker truce), identify stakeholder (Concord broker, Crown officer, Rootbound envoy, neutral magistrate), then pay one leverage cost (coin, favor, information, or risk acceptance). Reusable complications: forged waybills, embargo edicts, disappearances blamed on rivals, sabotage at lift pylons, and refugee leaders threatening mass walkout. Reward successful diplomacy with durable boons: reduced route danger for one leg, temporary neutrality writs, discounted resupply, or faction standing that converts into reinforcements in later regional crises.
